channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Grubhub to pay $25M to settle claims of tricking customers and drivers
item.description: Federal Trade Commission officials said Grubhub engaged in illegal practices by misleading customers about the cost of delivery and the benefits of a Grubhub subscription.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Biathlon official apologizes to athletes who have suffered mistreatment
item.description: The secretary general of the International Biathlon Union apologized to any athlete who was mistreated during his tenure at U.S. Biathlon while speaking to a 90-minute meeting Tuesday that focused on concerns about athlete safety brought to light by multiple Associated Press reports.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Massachusetts mother accused of strangling her 3 children seeks insanity defense
item.description: Lindsay Clancy, the Massachusetts mother accused of strangling her three young children to death before attempting to kill herself, is seeking an insanity defense, court records show.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man who killed brother and 3 others set to be put to death in Indiana's first execution in 15 years
item.description: MICHIGAN CITY, Indiana (AP) — An Indiana man convicted in the 1997 killings of his brother and three other men was set to receive a lethal injection by early Wednesday in the state's first execution in 15 years, without any independent witness present under the state's laws shielding information abo
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN and RICK CALLAHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Key details about the man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare's CEO
item.description: Luigi Mangione, the man accused of killing the CEO of UnitedHealthcare, was charged in New York with murder as an act of terrorism, prosecutors announced Tuesday. Mangione, 26, is being held without bail in Pennsylvania, where he was arrested at a McDonald’s on Dec.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE and SARAH BRUMFIELD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Indiana shrouds executions in secrecy, defying long tradition of public oversight
item.description: From public hangings in the town square to lethal injections witnessed by journalists, executions historically have mostly been carried out with at least some public scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ontario's premier says Trump's tariffs would be a disaster for US markets
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The premier of Canada's most populous province said Tuesday President-elect Donald Trump's plan to impose sweeping 25% tariffs on all Canadian products would be a "disaster" that would hurt U.S. stock markets.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Tom Cruise awarded highest Navy honor for a civilian
item.description: The 62-year-old actor was celebrated for his outstanding contributions to the Navy and the Marine Corps.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, NewsNation

item.title: Popular fishing YouTuber Ben Milliken arrested for fishing tournament fraud
item.description: According to TPWD, their due diligence process discovered that Milliken didn't have a valid fishing license.
item.dc:creator: Michael Garcia, NewsNation

item.title: New York to cap classroom temperatures next school year
item.description: New York schools have to follow a new law protecting students and staff from the heat starting September 1
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, NewsNation

item.title: Shooting in Madison is the latest carnage at a US school or college over the past 25 years
item.description: Hundreds of people in the U.S. have died in shootings over the past 25 years in places like offices, stores and theaters, but the carnage perhaps hits hardest when it happens in schools and colleges.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tom Cruise awarded highest Navy honor for a civilian
item.description: The 62-year-old actor was celebrated for his outstanding contributions to the Navy and the Marine Corps.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mystery drone sightings continue in New Jersey and across the US. Here's what we know
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (AP) — A large number of mysterious drones have been reported flying over New Jersey and across the eastern U.S., sparking speculation and concern over where they came from and why. New Jersey Gov. Phil Murphy and U.S. Sen.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Viral study on black kitchen utensils had glaring error, researchers issue correction
item.description: If you were caught up in last month's panic over black kitchen cooking utensils, you now have a reason to breathe easier.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tech consultant found guilty of second-degree murder in stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A San Francisco jury on Tuesday found a tech consultant guilty of second-degree murder in the stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee, which carries a sentence of 16 years to life, rejecting the defendant's claim that he had acted in self defense.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tax brackets and more are changing in 2024, 2025: What to know
item.description: We're more than 100 days out from Tax Day, but there are some impending updates you may want to prepare for.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump sues Des Moines Register, pollster for 'election interference' after pre-election poll
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump sued the Des Moines Register and its pollster for “brazen election interference” in publishing a survey the weekend before the election that showed Democrat Kamala Harris with a surprising lead of three percentage points in the state. The Register's parent Gannett Co.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NBA unveils new All-Star format featuring four-team tournament
item.description: The NBA 's All-Star Game is going to be an All-Star tournament this season, with the league announcing on Tuesday that it has finalized plans to use a different format for the upcoming midseason showcase in San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Parent attacks, strangles student in North Carolina high school hallway, officials say
item.description: "The parent did not report to the office and instead assaulted a student in the hallway," Renfrow said in the statement to families.
item.dc:creator: Harrison Grubb, NewsNation

item.title: What we know about the 2 killed, 6 injured in Wisconsin Christian school shooting
item.description: A student and teacher were killed and six others — five students and a teacher — were injured in Monday's shooting.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Suspect charged with killing UnitedHealthcare CEO as an act of terrorism, authorities announce
item.description: Mangione had previously been charged with murder in Thompson's killing, but the terror allegation is new.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Florida woman stole Christmas decorations, mail from storm-damaged homes: Police
item.description: A search of her car turned up "mail items containing personal identifying information, credit cards," as well as "several controlled substances," according to PCSO.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, NewsNation

item.title: Montana judge blocks rule that prevented transgender people from changing their sex on documents
item.description: HELENA, Mont. (AP) — A state judge in Montana has temporarily blocked policies that prevented transgender people from changing the sex designation on their birth certificates and driver’s licenses.
item.dc:creator: AMY BETH HANSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Family of slain Cash App founder Bob Lee reacts to Nima Momeni verdict
item.description: On Tuesday morning, a San Francisco jury announced it had found Nima Momeni guilty of second degree murder in the death of Cash App founder Bob Lee.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, Fox 5

item.title: Pee Dee Academy shuts down student section after racially charged incident
item.description: MULLINS, S.C. (WBTW) -- Pee Dee Academy officials are shutting down a cheering section in its gymnasium following an incident involving apparent racial insensitivity.
item.dc:creator: Adam Benson, NewsNation

item.title: Strange laws in New Mexico, including trouble for tripping a horse
item.description: You may be unaware you're breaking the law with the strange, widely unknown laws in U.S. states. A few of New Mexico's strangest include horses and duels.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: College hockey player sued by opponent over injury she suffered on the ice
item.description: A Michigan State University hockey player has been sued by a former opponent for injuries she suffered on the ice.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, KRON4

item.title: Taco Bell adding chicken nuggets to menus nationwide
item.description: Taco Bell, known for its Mexican-inspired cuisine, will begin serving its take on chicken nuggets later this month. Taco Bell’s Crispy Chicken Nuggets features tortilla breading and will be served with three sauces, including the first-ever Hidden Valley Fire Ranch.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: A timeline of the investigation of the Gilgo Beach killings
item.description: For years, women had been disappearing on Long Island. Then in 2010, investigators searching for a missing woman began finding 10 sets of human remains in the scrub along a barrier island parkway, not far from the sands of New York's remote Gilgo Beach.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Grammy award-winning gospel singer arrested in Greenville
item.description: GREENVILLE, S.C. (WSPA) - Grammy award-winning gospel singer Le'Andria Johnson was arrested Monday afternoon in Greenville for public intoxication. The City of Greenville confirmed with 7NEWS that Johnson was charged with public intoxication and an open container on a public street.
item.dc:creator: Nikolette Miller, Fox 5

item.title: Suspect in Gilgo Beach serial killings is charged in the death of a seventh woman
item.description: The New York architect facing murder charges in a string of deaths known as the Gilgo Beach killings was charged on Tuesday in the death of a seventh woman.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press via Nexstar Media Wire, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Alabama woman doing well after experimental pig kidney transplant
item.description: Towana Looney is the fifth American given a gene-edited pig organ — and notably, she isn’t as sick as prior recipients who died within two months of receiving a pig kidney or heart.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Suspected serial killer Rex Heuermann charged with seventh slaying
item.description: Gilgo Beach murder suspect Rex Heuermann has been charged with a seventh slaying, in connection with the death of Philadelphia woman Valerie Mack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump tariff plan threatens to drive up gas prices
item.description: Trump said that on his first day in office, he would place 25 percent tariffs on all Canadian and Mexican goods. The two countries are the leading sources of U.S. oil imports.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WGN 9
